title: Senior Epidemiologist, Immunology, RWE (FSP Sponsor Dedicated)
description:
Location: Remote, candidates should preferably be based in Spain, Portugal or UK (o*ther EU locations may be considered*).


Join IQVIA’s Sponsor\-Dedicated FSP team and help advance healthcare through real\-world evidence. We bring together scientific expertise and real\-world data to help sponsors make decisions and accelerate evidence generation to improve patient outcomes. In this role, you’ll work closely with one sponsor, gaining deep knowledge of their therapies while leveraging IQVIA’s global expertise.


**Core Function Description:**


This role will support the Inflammation, Real World Evidence team, providing scientific and strategic epidemiology support for clinical development and drug safety activities. This role will leverage secondary real\-world data (RWD) sources to generate evidence that informs regulatory, safety, and clinical development decisions across the product lifecycle.

  

  

Key responsibilities include designing and conducting observational research studies, assessing evidence gaps, evaluating safety profiles, and developing evidence strategies to support regulatory interactions and clinical development programs. The Senior Epidemiologist will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Clinical Development, Safety, Regulatory Affairs, Biostatistics, and Real\-World Evidence stakeholders, to ensure appropriate epidemiologic approaches are applied to address critical business and scientific questions.  



  

The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in epidemiologic methods, demonstrated experience working within pharma or biotech, and a thorough understanding of regulatory requirements related to clinical development and safety evidence generation.


This is a fully remote position based in the United States.


**Minimum Qualifications**


* Doctoral degree (PhD, ScD, or equivalent) in Epidemiology.
* 5 to 7 years of epidemiology experience within apharmaceutical/biotechnologycompany.
* Demonstrated experience supporting clinical development and/or drug safety.
* Strong expertise in observational research methods and the use of commercial healthcare databases such as HealthVerity, Pharmetrics Plus, TriNetX, or similar real\-world data assets.
* Solid understanding of regulatory requirements and evidence needs supporting clinical development, including the generation of evidence to address regulatory questions related to safety profiles and evidence gaps for Phase II and Phase III programs.
* Proven ability to design, execute, and interpret epidemiologic studies that support regulatory, clinical, or safety objectives.

**Preferred Qualifications**


* Experience in Inflammation and Immunology therapeutic areas.
* Knowledge of disease areas such as Atopic Dermatitis, Asthma, Lupus, Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP), Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia, or other immune\-mediated diseases.
* Experience supporting assets within emerging immunology programs.
* Experience working with primary or hybrid real\-world data sources in addition to secondary data sources.
* Prior experience supporting regulatory submissions or interactions with FDA, EMA, or other global health authorities.
**What’s in it for you?**


* Be part of a forward\-thinking team that helps shape the next generation of evidence\-based healthcare.
* Work hand\-in\-hand with one leading sponsor, gaining deep expertise in their therapies.
* Access IQVIA’s global network who supports your growth.

This is your chance to make an impact, while building a career that matters.


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com


IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.
company_description: IQVIA (NYSE:IQV) is a leading global provider of advanced analytics, technology solutions and clinical research services to the life sciences industry dedicated to delivering actionable insights.

title: Administrative Coordinator
description:
Why Join Us:
**Why You’ll Love Working at Cook**  

At Cook, we believe in taking care of our people so they can take care of others. As a global, family\-owned company that has been improving patients’ lives since 1963, we’re committed to doing business with integrity and making a positive impact in the communities where we live and work.


We’re guided by strong ethical standards and core values, and we believe the best ideas come from creating solutions together. Our company culture values every voice, brings together inclusive teams who collaborate well, and encourages learning and innovation. When you join Cook, you’re not just starting a job, you’re building a career with purpose, in a company that invests in you.

**What You Can Expect at Cook**  

These are some ways we support our people across Cook:

* **Meaningful work \& strong values:** Contribute to improving patient care globally in a company known for high ethical standards and integrity.
* **Competitive rewards \& security:** Competitive, performance\-related compensation and benefits that support your health and financial wellbeing.
* **Flexibility \& time off:** Paid time off, holidays, and hybrid or remote work options for many jobs, where possible.
* **Growth \& development:** Learning opportunities, educational assistance, and development programs to help you grow your skills and career.
* **Wellbeing \& community:** Health and wellness initiatives, social activities, and community engagement that help you feel connected and supported.


Benefits and programs vary by country and job. Our recruiters can provide more information about the specific benefits and programs available in your location.


Overview:

The Administrative Coordinator provides administrative and coordination support across compliance, HR administration, legal, and health and safety activities, helping to ensure the smooth day\-to\-day operation of these functions. The role supports compliance with Italian regulations and works closely with Sustainability initiatives to coordinate related administrative processes.  

Acting as a key point of contact between local operations, Managing Directors, external advisors, and global departments, the Administrative Coordinator facilitates communication, coordinates administrative activities, and helps ensure that business processes are completed accurately, efficiently, and in line with company policies and local requirements. Regular attendance at the office is required in accordance with business needs.
Responsibilities:
**General Affairs \& HR Administration*** Support local employee benefits programmes in collaboration with HR, payroll provider and external benefit providers.
* Support HR function with the preparation and administration of employment documentation.
* Ensure compliance with Italian Health \& Safety legislation by coordinating with external consultants, including the RSPP, Radiation Protection Expert, and Competent Doctor.
* Prepare employee accreditation and authorisation documentation required for access to hospitals and customer sites.
* Manage and monitor the company's Certified Email (PEC) mailbox, ensuring the timely processing and distribution of internal and external communications.

**Legal \& Compliance*** Support the implementation and ongoing maintenance of local governance and compliance frameworks in accordance with Italian legislation, including Legislative Decree 231/2001 and Anti\-Bribery Law 190/2012, in collaboration with Legal and Ethics \& Compliance teams.
* Coordinate and support activities relating to Supervisory Body (Organismo di Vigilanza) meetings, audits, and inspections, ensuring the timely preparation of required documentation.
* Monitor changes in relevant legislation, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices, keeping local leadership informed of potential business impacts.
* Partner with the Governance \& Contracts team to support the preparation, review, and administration of local agency and distribution agreements.

**Sustainability \& Development**  

* Monitor developments in sustainability legislation, environmental regulations, and ESG best practices to ensure ongoing compliance and identify opportunities for improvement.

  

* Gather and analyse feedback from employees, customers, and industry associations to support the development of sustainability initiatives.

  

* Identify and recommend sustainability projects aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

  

* Promote a culture of continuous improvement by supporting local sustainability programmes and cross\-functional initiatives.

**Fleet Management**

* Manage the company vehicle fleet, including vehicle allocation, leasing contracts, renewals, and end\-of\-lease returns.
* Act as the primary point of contact with the leasing provider (Ayvens), negotiating terms and ensuring cost\-effective fleet solutions.
* Monitor fleet performance, including vehicle utilisation, maintenance schedules, servicing, and insurance coverage.
* Ensure full compliance with local legislation, health and safety requirements, and company fleet policies.
* Prepare regular fleet reports and provide data\-driven insights and recommendations to improve operational efficiency, optimise costs, and support informed decision\-making.
* Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external suppliers to ensure the effective and seamless management of fleet operations.


Qualifications:  

* Bachelor’s degree in business administration, legal, or a related field.
* Strong knowledge of Italian tax, accounting, and employment legislation.
* Previous experience in HR administration and/or compliance roles within multinational environments.
* Excellent organizational, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Proven ability to collaborate effectively across cross\-functional global teams and with external advisors.
* Proactive, detail\-oriented mindset with a strong interest in sustainability, process improvement, and continuous learning.
* Fluent Italian with an excellent command of spoken and written English.

title: Medical Manager
description:
RESPONSIBILITIES/DUTIES  

  



* Implement the Italian Medical Affairs activities in alignment with regional and global medical strategies and local marketing and sales plans
* Provide scientific and medical expertise across therapy areas and product portfolio and pipeline products
* Identify medical needs, scientific developments and emerging clinical trends relevant to Orion's products and therapeutic areas
* Ensure medical activities support business objectives while maintaining the highest ethical and compliance standards
* Timely dissemination of updates and information relating to medical and scientific matters to relevant parties
* Act as the primary medical contact for the Italian affiliate for internal customers and external customers (e.g HCPs)
* Work closely with Commercial, Marketing, Regulatory , Market access teams to support product launches, lifecycle management initiatives and brand activities from a medical perspective to shape brand and campaign plans and support the development of sales and marketing materials as appropriate
* Provide the medical review and approval of promotional and non\-promotional materials
* Create and maintain relationships with KOLs and HCPs as necessary
* Represent Orion at scientific congresses, advisory boards and medical meetings
* Collaborate with global medical colleagues to ensure alignment of medical activities and priorities
* Develop and maintain relationships with Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs), healthcare professionals, medical societies and other relevant stakeholders
* Ensure that all medical activities comply with applicable Italian regulations, AIFA requirements, EFPIA/Farmindustria Code requirements and Orion policies
* Support pharmacovigilance processes, ensuring the appropriate handling and reporting of adverse events and product quality complaints.
* Contribute to the development, maintenance and continuous improvement of medical procedures and SOPs.
* Support audits, inspections and compliance initiatives when required.  

  

  

  

QUALIFICATIONS \& EXPERIENCE:
  

  


	+ University degree in Medicine, Pharmacy, Life Sciences or a related scientific discipline
	+ Good Experience within Medical Affairs in the Italian pharmaceutical industry
	+ Experience working with healthcare professionals and key external stakeholders
	+ Experience working cross\-functionally in a matrix environment
	+ Experience on branded products (generic and not generics) in retail and Hospital channelTECHNICAL SKILLS AND ABILITIES:
  

  


	+ Scientific and medical knowledge
	+ Excellent communication and presentation skills
	+ Ability to translate complex scientific information for different audiences
	+ Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills
	+ Ability to build credible relationships with KOLs and healthcare professionals
	+ Project management and organisational capabilities
	+ Good understanding of pharmaceutical industry compliance requirements and medical governance
	+ Ability to analyze medical insights and convert them into actionable recommendations
	+ Fluently in English and ItalianCOMPETENCIES:
  

  


	+ Collaborative and team\-oriented
	+ Curious and eager to learn
	+ Strong ethical judgement and integrity
	+ Proactive and solution\-focused
	+ Resilient and adaptable
	+ Comfortable working independently and taking ownership
	+ Effective coach and people developer
	+ Strong interpersonal and networking skills
	+ Patient\-focused mindsetCONTRACTUAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS:
  

  

**Employment Type:** Permanent employment contract under the Chemical and Pharmaceutical Industry Collective Agreement (CCNL)
  

**Job Classification:** Quadro
  

**Benefits:** Company car, laptop, mobile phone
  

**Start Date:** As soon as possible

title: Coordinatore tecnico
description:
FE\-Group Srl, gruppo industriale italiano in continua crescita, specializzato nella progettazione, produzione e commercializzazione di dispositivi e strumenti per il settore medicale\-oftalmico, ricerca un/una **Coordinatore Ufficio Tecnico e Industrializzazione da inserire presso la propria sede di Solbiate Olona (VA).**

**La posizione**

La risorsa avrà il compito di coordinare e organizzare le attività dell’Ufficio Tecnico, con particolare attenzione all’industrializzazione, alla standardizzazione dei processi e alla gestione integrata dei dati tecnici di prodotto.

Uno degli obiettivi prioritari sarà armonizzare il patrimonio tecnico delle diverse linee di prodotto del Gruppo, intervenendo su codifiche, anagrafiche articoli, disegni, distinte base, cicli produttivi, documentazione tecnica e ricambi.

La persona collaborerà direttamente con la Direzione, il Responsabile di Produzione e i responsabili delle diverse funzioni aziendali, rappresentando il **collegamento operativo tra Ufficio Tecnico, Ricerca e Sviluppo, Produzione, Acquisti, Qualità, Magazzino e Assistenza Tecnica.**

**Principali attività**

* Coordinare e organizzare le attività dell’Ufficio Tecnico, definendo priorità, responsabilità e modalità operative.
* Coordinare le risorse interne ed eventuali collaboratori esterni coinvolti nei progetti tecnici.
* Definire criteri comuni per la codifica, classificazione e gestione dei dati tecnici delle diverse linee di prodotto.
* Creare, aggiornare e standardizzare anagrafiche articoli, distinte base multilivello, cicli produttivi e configurazioni di prodotto.
* Organizzare e mantenere aggiornati disegni, modelli 3D, tavole tecniche, schemi elettrici ed elettronici, manuali, schede tecniche e documentazione di produzione.
* Gestire le revisioni dei prodotti e le modifiche tecniche, assicurando la corretta tracciabilità delle informazioni.
* Armonizzare le distinte di produzione con le distinte ricambi e con le esigenze del servizio di assistenza tecnica.
* Assicurare la coerenza tra documentazione tecnica, sistema gestionale ERP e processi operativi aziendali.
* Strutturare e mantenere ordinati gli archivi tecnici digitali, introducendo metodologie di gestione documentale e sistemi PDM/PLM, ove opportuno.
* Collaborare con il Responsabile di Produzione per la risoluzione delle problematiche tecniche, il miglioramento dei processi e il rispetto delle priorità produttive.
* Supportare l’industrializzazione dei nuovi prodotti, dalla prototipazione e validazione fino alla messa in produzione.
* Collaborare con le funzioni Qualità e Regulatory Affairs per garantire l’allineamento della documentazione tecnica dei dispositivi.
* Promuovere attività di standardizzazione, razionalizzazione dei componenti, riduzione dei costi e miglioramento continuo.
* Coordinare operativamente progetti tecnici trasversali riguardanti i diversi brand e prodotti del Gruppo.

La risorsa potrà inoltre essere coinvolta in ulteriori progetti di carattere tecnico, produttivo e industriale, contribuendo direttamente allo sviluppo organizzativo del Gruppo.

**Requisiti richiesti**

* Diploma tecnico o laurea in ambito elettronico, elettrotecnico, meccatronico, automazione, meccanico o industriale.
* Esperienza quinquennale all’interno di un Ufficio Tecnico di un’azienda manifatturiera, preferibilmente maturata su prodotti elettromeccanici complessi.
* Solida conoscenza di distinte base, cicli produttivi, codifiche, anagrafiche articoli e sistemi gestionali ERP.
* Capacità di leggere e interpretare disegni meccanici, schemi elettrici ed elettronici e documentazione tecnica di prodotto.
* Buona conoscenza dei software CAD 3D e capacità di realizzare o modificare modelli, assiemi e tavole tecniche.
* Conoscenza dei processi produttivi e delle principali attività di industrializzazione.
* Esperienza nella gestione delle revisioni, delle modifiche tecniche e della documentazione controllata.
* Capacità di analisi, pianificazione e organizzazione.
* Approccio concreto e operativo, capacità di problem solving e orientamento ai risultati.
* Buone capacità relazionali e attitudine al lavoro con funzioni aziendali differenti.
* Buona conoscenza dell’inglese tecnico.

**Requisiti preferenziali**

* Formazione elettronica, elettrotecnica o meccatronica.
* Esperienza su strumenti o prodotti che integrano meccanica, elettronica, ottica e software.
* Conoscenza di IronCAD o disponibilità ad apprenderne rapidamente l’utilizzo.
* Esperienza con sistemi PDM o PLM.
* Esperienza nella riorganizzazione o migrazione di dati tecnici provenienti da differenti linee di prodotto o realtà aziendali.
* Esperienza nel settore dei dispositivi medicali, delle apparecchiature elettromedicali o in aziende manifatturiere ad alta specializzazione.
* Conoscenza dei sistemi qualità e dei requisiti documentali applicabili ai dispositivi medici.

**Cosa offriamo**

* Un ruolo centrale nel progetto di integrazione e sviluppo industriale del Gruppo.
* Rapporto diretto con la Direzione e con i responsabili delle principali funzioni aziendali.
* Autonomia nell’organizzazione delle attività dell’Ufficio Tecnico.
* Possibilità di lavorare su prodotti medicali complessi che integrano meccanica, elettronica, ottica e software.
* Partecipazione diretta ai progetti di sviluppo, industrializzazione e miglioramento dei prodotti.
* Ambiente dinamico, innovativo e orientato al miglioramento continuo.
* Concrete opportunità di crescita professionale e di responsabilità.
* Inquadramento e retribuzione commisurati all’esperienza e alle competenze della persona individuata.
* Contratto di lavoro full time.
* Attività prevalentemente in presenza presso la sede aziendale di Solbiate Olona (VA).

La ricerca è rivolta a persone di ogni genere, età e nazionalità, nel rispetto della normativa vigente.

title: Project Manager – Consultant
Local Study Associate Director CAR-T
description:
**Location** Milan, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-256075 **Date posted** 12/07/2026
  
Are you ready to lead country\-level CAR\-T studies that turn scientific ambition into outcomes for patients with speed and precision? In this role, you will convert complex cell therapy strategies into operational excellence, accelerating start\-up, elevating site performance, and safeguarding quality across every milestone. You will partner closely with cross\-functional experts—monitoring, regulatory, medical, data and quality—to deliver studies that are inspection\-ready and on time. Your leadership will strengthen site relationships, shorten activation timelines, and improve data integrity, directly influencing how quickly transformative therapies reach those who need them. Based in Milan, you will orchestrate delivery in a fast\-moving environment where smart decisions and disciplined execution matter. Can you bring clarity, pace and accountability to high\-stakes studies while coaching others to raise the bar?


**Accountabilities**:


**Leadership and Oversight**: Lead the Local Study Team, set a clear operating rhythm, and optimize team performance to deliver country milestones on time and within budget; ensure compliance and effective governance, and run high\-value meetings that drive decisions and momentum.


**Study Management:** Translate protocol and strategy into country execution plans; develop monitoring strategies, coordinate timely site identification and selection, and secure on\-time submissions to enable rapid activation and sustained site performance.


**Feasibility and Site Engagement:** Lead country feasibility assessments, leveraging knowledge of the local clinical research landscape to identify high\-potential sites and assess patient recruitment opportunities. Partner closely with investigators and site staff to evaluate study viability, anticipate enrollment challenges, and shape execution plans that maximize study delivery and patient access. Contribute strategic insights to study allocation decisions and support the development of strong relationships with key research centers.


**Monitoring and Compliance:** Conduct co\-monitoring to strengthen quality and coaching, review monitoring outputs to spot trends early, and oversee audit activities; drive corrective and preventive actions that keep studies inspection\-ready.


**Documentation and Systems:** Maintain complete, accurate documentation throughout the study lifecycle; ensure systems are current and reliable and prepare for final archiving to protect data integrity and regulatory readiness.


**Collaboration and Reporting**: Work hand\-in\-hand with CRAs, Investigators and Regulatory Affairs; provide clear, regular updates on progress, issues and decisions to keep stakeholders aligned and risks managed.


**Training and Support:** Onboard and coach new team members; champion process improvements that simplify operations, reduce cycle times and scale best practice across studies.


**Regional Contribution:** Uphold company values and contribute to regional initiatives that harmonize ways of working and elevate performance beyond a single study.


**Essential Skills/Experience:**


* Proven ability to lead a Local Study Team, optimize team performance, ensure compliance, organize meetings and manage budgets.
* Experience meeting study commitments, developing monitoring plans, coordinating site selection and ensuring timely document submissions.
* Experience performing co\-monitoring visits, reviewing monitoring reports and overseeing audit activities.
* Demonstrated capability to ensure document completeness, maintain study information in required systems and prepare for final archiving.
* Strong track record of working closely with CRAs, Investigators and Regulatory Affairs, providing updates on study progress.
* Training and Support: Experience training new team members and contributing to process improvements.
* Miscellaneous: Commitment to company values and willingness to support regional initiatives.

**Desirable Skills/Experience:**


* Experience leading country\-level studies in oncology or other complex therapeutic areas. Familiarity with risk\-based monitoring and data\-driven site management approaches.
* Relevant experience on management of CAR\-T Cell Therapy studies.
* Proficiency with clinical trial systems (e.g., CTMS, eTMF) and documentation standards.
* Demonstrated success in audit readiness and inspection management.
* Strong stakeholder management skills with investigators, site networks and regulatory partners.
* Experience driving cross\-study process improvements and harmonization across regions.

**Why AstraZeneca**: Join a team advancing cancer care from early diagnosis through long\-term survivorship, where science, speed and rigorous delivery come together to change what’s possible for patients. We unite diverse specialists to make fast, data\-informed decisions, pairing bold ideas with disciplined execution. Here you will shape how innovative combinations and next\-wave modalities like CAR\-T reach patients sooner, while growing as a leader in a culture that balances ambition with kindness. We believe the best work happens when people are together, so we typically meet in the office at least three days a week—creating the energy to co\-create, challenge assumptions and move faster—while offering flexibility when it matters.


**Call to Action:** If you are ready to lead local study delivery from Milan and turn breakthrough science into tangible patient benefit, take the next step and help us accelerate what’s possible!


**We offer:**


Salary range: 64\.206,40€ to 96\.309,60€ per annum


Contract Level: A3


CBA: Chemical\-Pharmaceutical


Full\-time


Permanent


**Benefits:**


**In addition to the Gross Base Salary, we offer:**


* An annual target bonus governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review/Sales incentives governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review
* Participation Bonus
* Wellbeing benefits (welfare, insurance, psychological support, etc.)

Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to skills, experience, internal organizational equity and other relevant factor.

13\-lug\-2026
**Closing Date**


27\-lug\-2026

We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry\-leading skills. We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non\-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

title: Regulatory Affairs (stage/intern)
SE Medical Cluster Lead
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.
  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.
  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.


The SE Medical Cluster Lead is accountable for the execution of Europe‑level Medical \& Scientific Affairs strategy across the SE cluster.


The role ensures consistent, high‑quality, and compliant medical delivery aligned to Haleon’s Trusted Science principles, while acting as the primary local medical interface to cluster commercial, regulatory organisations and external stakeholders.


This role focuses on translation, deployment, and execution of category medical strategies, evidence frameworks, and governance standards, while feeding local insights, risks, and opportunities back into Europe Medical leadership.


**Role Responsibilities**

**Medical Execution \& Local Delivery**

* Execute approved Europe category medical strategies (OTC, Oral Health, Wellness) at cluster level, ensuring consistent implementation across markets.
* Provide medical leadership to support launch excellence, lifecycle management, and in‑market optimisation, in line with category priorities and approved frameworks.
* Act as the local medical partner to Commercial cluster leadership, enabling confident, risk‑aware decision‑making.

**External Scientific Engagement**

* Serve as Haleon’s senior local medical representative within the cluster, engaging with healthcare professionals, scientific experts, and relevant external stakeholders.
* Lead local execution of scientific exchange, medical education, and non‑promotional expert engagement in alignment with Europe Medical engagement frameworks.
* Identify and escalate emerging scientific, reputational, or external risks and opportunities to Europe Medical leadership.

**Governance \& Compliance**

* Ensure all cluster medical activities are conducted in accordance with Europe Medical Governance frameworks, SOPs, and regulatory requirements.
* Proactively identify, manage, and escalate medical and compliance risks through established governance channels.
* Support audit readiness and inspection activities at cluster level.

**Evidence Deployment \& Scientific Quality**

* Deploy approved evidence packages, scientific narratives, and claims frameworks to support local medical and commercial needs.
* Ensure accurate, compliant use of evidence in all local medical activities and materials.
* Provide structured feedback to the Europe Evidence \& Scientific Excellence function on local evidence gaps, insights, and execution challenges.

**Cross** **‑** **Functional Collaboration**

* Work in close partnership with Europe Category Medical Leads, Medical Governance, Medical Evidence \& Scientific Excellence, Regulatory, Safety, and Commercial teams.
* Translate Europe‑level guidance into clear, actionable local medical recommendations.
* Act as the escalation point for cluster‑specific issues requiring Europe‑level resolution.

**Qualifications \& experience**

**Basic Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in Life Sciences (Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, or equivalent required).
* 5 or more years of industry experience in Medical Affairs within OTC/consumer healthcare or other regulated environments.
* Proven track record of translating regional or global medical strategies into effective local execution.
* Strong experience in external scientific engagement and cross\-functional collaboration.
* People leadership experience in a matrix organization.
* Multi\-lingual (English and Italian)

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Advanced scientific or clinical qualification preferred (PhD, MD, PharmD, MSc, or equivalent).
* Strong scientific judgment with a practical execution mindset.
* High integrity and confidence operating within governance frameworks.
* Ability to challenge constructively, escalate risks, and influence without authority.
* Collaborative, matrix\-savvy leader with strong stakeholder management skills.
* Knowledge and understanding of consumer needs and attitudes, customer groups, and opinion leaders
* Cooperation and teamwork with R\&D, Marketing and other internal partners.
Comfortable challenging others, being challenged, and fostering healthy debate over important issues.

description:
Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.



We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again.



We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including: Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection.



At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential.

  


**Scope of Role:**



Focal point within Mercury for all project issues related to environment and sustainability.

  


**Key Responsibilities of the Role:**


* Manage and track compliance with all environmental approvals, licences, and permits relating to the site.
* Provide advice and input into the identification and effective management of environmental risks.
* Support the development, implementation, and ongoing management of project environmental management systems
* Support the maintenance of comprehensive environmental records.
* Liaise with government bodies and professional organisations on environmental matters as required.
* Manage environmental consultants and undertake field monitoring as required.
* Conclusion and submission of required regulatory paperwork
* Drive/manage Environmental (awareness) training for all project personnel (toolbox talks, etc)
* Monitoring and supervising the correct implementation of the Environmental \& LEED Management Plans (Pollution Prevention Plan, Waste Management Plan, Indoor Air Quality MP, etc). Ensuring all inspection related are carried out at an appropriate frequency and that sufficient information is being gathered and actioned where required.
* Interfaces with internal and external team members, including clients, project managers, architects, engineers, contractors, and commissioning agents.
* Coordinates with Mercury Environmental/Sustainability Manager to ensure internal goals and processes are met.
* Focal point for The Sustainability Tool \-Waste tracker.
* LEED: Conducts technical reviews of material and construction documentation for quality assurance and assuring required certificates (EPD, VOCs, Recycled contents) are gathered in line with LEED scorecard.
* LEED: Communicates with third\-party certifiers (e.g., GBCI, The GBI) throughout the review process, when needed.
* LEED: Overall supervision of LEED process on the construction side and ensuring that the points on LEED scorecard are achieved where possible.
* LEED: Develop Monthly LEED Report to inform progress on the certification process.
* Any other duties associated with this role

  


**Core Competencies:**


* Self\-motivated and can work efficiently in an independent role within a group to achieve goals Adaptability, Collaboration, Communication etc.
* Have excellent time management skills and are mindful of deadlines.
* Adaptability, Collaboration, Communication etc

  


**Essential Criteria:**


* Degree in Engineering, science or related discipline, Masters preferred.
* The ideal candidate will have a minimum of 5 years of work experience with environmental\-HSE related subjects.
* Previous experience in Construction Operations.
* Fluent in English (written and spoken) and local language of the project (plus).
* Proactive and open team player.

  


**Desirable Criteria:**


* LEED GA or LEED AP certificate.
* Knowledge of Revit, or any modelling programme (Navisworks, etc)
* ACC or similar.

  


***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer***.

  


**Ingegnere ambientale, Milano**

  



Mercury è leader europeo nelle soluzioni per l'edilizia.



Progettiamo e gestiamo complessi progetti di ingegneria e costruzione per le aziende leader a livello mondiale. Le nostre soluzioni contribuiscono a fornire tecnologie e innovazioni rivoluzionarie che connettono persone, comunità e imprese, dando loro il potere di realizzare cose straordinarie. La nostra gente ha il coraggio di essere innovativa. La loro determinazione e la loro grande concentrazione ci permettono di ottenere risultati con certezza, ogni volta.



Realizziamo le visioni dei nostri clienti attraverso soluzioni di costruzione all'avanguardia in molteplici settori, tra cui: data center aziendali, tecnologie avanzate e scienze della vita, data center hyperscale e protezione antincendio.



In Mercury, è nostro dovere incoraggiare e supportare i nostri collaboratori affinché realizzino la loro visione di sé stessi. Li mettiamo al centro di tutto ciò che facciamo, offrendo loro opportunità stimolanti per crescere all'interno di un team affiatato e in un ambiente di supporto che permetta loro di esprimere appieno il proprio potenziale.

  


**Ambito del ruolo:**



Punto di riferimento all'interno di Mercury per tutte le questioni progettuali relative all'ambiente e alla sostenibilità.

  


**Principali responsabilità del ruolo:**


* Gestire e monitorare la conformità a tutte le autorizzazioni, licenze e permessi ambientali relativi al sito.
* Fornire consulenza e supporto per l'identificazione e la gestione efficace dei rischi ambientali.
* Sostenere lo sviluppo, l'implementazione e la gestione continua dei sistemi di gestione ambientale del progetto.
* Sostenere la tenuta di una documentazione ambientale completa.
* Collaborare con enti governativi e organizzazioni professionali su questioni ambientali, secondo necessità.
* Gestire i consulenti ambientali ed effettuare il monitoraggio sul campo secondo necessità.
* Completamento e presentazione della documentazione normativa richiesta
* Gestire/promuovere la formazione in materia ambientale (di sensibilizzazione) per tutto il personale del progetto (riunioni di sicurezza, ecc.).
* Monitoraggio e supervisione della corretta implementazione dei piani di gestione ambientale e LEED (Piano di prevenzione dell'inquinamento, Piano di gestione dei rifiuti, Piano di gestione della qualità dell'aria interna, ecc.). Garantire che tutte le ispezioni siano effettuate con una frequenza adeguata e che vengano raccolte informazioni sufficienti e che si agisca di conseguenza, ove necessario.
* Interagisce con i membri del team interni ed esterni, inclusi clienti, project manager, architetti, ingegneri, appaltatori e responsabili del collaudo.
* Collabora con il responsabile ambientale/di sostenibilità di Mercury per garantire il rispetto degli obiettivi e dei processi interni.
* Punto di riferimento per lo strumento di sostenibilità: il sistema di monitoraggio dei rifiuti.
* LEED: Effettua revisioni tecniche della documentazione relativa ai materiali e alla costruzione per garantire la qualità e assicurarsi che i certificati richiesti (EPD, VOC, contenuto riciclato) siano raccolti in conformità con la scorecard LEED.
* LEED: Comunica con gli enti di certificazione terzi (ad es. GBCI, The GBI) durante tutto il processo di revisione, quando necessario.
* LEED: Supervisione generale del processo LEED dal punto di vista della costruzione e garanzia del raggiungimento, ove possibile, dei punteggi previsti dalla scorecard LEED.
* LEED: Elaborare un report mensile LEED per informare sullo stato di avanzamento del processo di certificazione.
* Qualsiasi altro compito associato a questo ruolo

  


**Competenze chiave:**


* Persona automotivata, capace di lavorare in modo efficiente e autonomo all'interno di un gruppo per raggiungere gli obiettivi. Adattabilità, collaborazione, comunicazione, ecc.
* Possiedo eccellenti capacità di gestione del tempo e rispetto le scadenze.
* Adattabilità, collaborazione, comunicazione ecc.

  


**Criteri essenziali :**


* Laurea in ingegneria, scienze o discipline affini, preferibilmente con master.
* Il candidato ideale avrà un'esperienza lavorativa di almeno 5 anni in materie relative alla salute, sicurezza e ambiente (HSE).
* Precedente esperienza nelle operazioni di costruzione.
* Ottima conoscenza dell'inglese (scritto e parlato) e della lingua locale del progetto (requisito preferenziale).
* Persona proattiva e aperta, capace di lavorare in team.

  


**Criteri desiderabili :**


* Certificazione LEED GA o LEED AP.
* Conoscenza di Revit o di qualsiasi altro programma di modellazione (Navisworks, ecc.).
* ACC o similare.

  


***Mercury è un datore di lavoro che offre pari opportunità.***.

title: Sr CRA II - FSP Team
description:
**Job Overview:**

The Senior Clinical Research Associate II (Sr CRA II) is responsible and accountable for site monitoring and site management for clinical studies according to Fortrea, and/or Sponsor, Standard Operating Procedures, and Regulatory Guidelines. The Sr CRA II assures the implementation of project plans as assigned, functions as leader for projects of limited scope, as assigned and assumes line management responsibilities, as assigned. Acts in the project role of a Local Project Coordinator or Lead CRA as assigned.


**Summary of Responsibilities:**

* The statements below are intended to describe the general nature of the job and are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, skills, and duties.
* Responsible for all aspects of study site monitoring including routine monitoring and close\-out of clinical sites, maintenance of study files, conduct of pre\-study and initiation visits; liaise with vendors; and other duties, as assigned.
* Responsible for all aspects of site management as prescribed in the project plans.
* Ensure the study staff who will conduct the protocol have received the proper materials and instructions to safely enter patients into the study.
* Ensure the protection of study patients by verifying that informed consent procedures and protocol requirements are adhered to according to the applicable regulatory requirements.
* Ensure the integrity of the data submitted on Case Report Forms (CRFs) or other data collection tools by careful source document review.
* Monitor data for missing or implausible data.
* Ensure the resources of the Sponsor and Fortrea are spent wisely by performing the required monitoring tasks in an efficient manner, according to SOPs and established guidelines, including managing travel expenses in an economical fashion according to Fortrea travel policy.
* Ensure audit readiness at the site level.
* Travel, including air travel, may be required and is an essential function of the job.
* Prepare accurate and timely trip reports.
* Manage small projects under the direction of a Project Manager/Director as assigned.
* Serve as lead monitor for a protocol or project and may assist in establishing monitoring plans and trip report review as assigned.
* Review progress of projects and initiate appropriate actions to achieve target objectives.
* Organize and make presentations at Investigator Meetings.
* Participate in the development of protocols and Case Report Forms as assigned.
* Participate in writing clinical trial reports as assigned.
* Interact with internal work groups to evaluate needs, resources and timelines.
* Act as contact for clinical trial supplies and other suppliers (vendors) as assigned.
* Responsible for all aspects of registry management as prescribed in the project plans.
* Undertake feasibility work when requested.
* Conduct, report, and follow\-up on Quality Control (QC) visits when requested.
* Recruitment of potential investigators, preparation of EC submissions, notifications to regulatory authorities, translation of study\-related documentation, organization of meetings and other tasks as instructed by supervisor as assigned.
* Negotiate study budgets with potential investigators and assist the Fortrea legal department with statements of agreements as assigned.
* Track and follow\-up on Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting, process production of reports, narratives and follow up of SAEs.
* Independently perform CRF review; query generation and resolution against established data review guidelines on Fortrea or client data management systems as assigned by management.
* Assist with training, mentoring and development of new employees, e.g., co\-monitoring.
* Coordinate designated clinical projects as a Local Project Coordinator (with supervision, if applicable), and may act as a local client contact as assigned.
* Perform other duties as needed or assigned.

**Qualifications (Minimum Required):**

* University or college degree, or certification in a related allied health profession from an appropriately accredited institution (e.g., nursing licensure), an equivalent amount of experience can be substituted as appropriate.
* In lieu of the above education requirement, candidates with a minimum of 6 years recent clinical monitoring experience will be considered.
* Thorough knowledge of regulatory requirements.
* Thorough understanding of the drug development process.
* Fluent in local office language and in English, both written and verbal.
* Or an equivalent combination of education and experience to successfully perform the key responsibilities of the job.
* Fortrea may consider relevant and equivalent experience in lieu of educational requirement.

**Experience (Minimum Required):**

* Three\-Four (3\-4\) years of Clinical Monitoring experience
* Have a full understanding of Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting, process production of reports, narratives and follow up of SAEs.
* Advanced site monitoring skills.
* Advanced study site management skills.
* Advanced registry administration skills.
* Ability to work with minimal supervision.
* Good planning and organization skills.
* Good computer skills with good working knowledge of a range of computer packages
* Advanced verbal and written communication skills.
* Ability to train and supervise junior staff.
* Ability to resolve project\-related problems and prioritizes workload for self and team.
* Ability to work within a project team.
* Works efficiently and effectively in a matrix environment.

**Preferred Qualifications Include:**

* Thorough knowledge of company SOPs regarding site monitoring.
* Minimum 1 Year experience in a related field (i.e., medical, clinical, pharmaceutical, laboratory, research, data analysis, data management or technical writing) is preferred.
* Local project coordination and/or project management experience.
* Ability to maintain confidentiality of data and information during interactions with staff at all levels and across studies and sponsors.
* Demonstrated ability to conduct clinical operations activities most effectively and efficiently.
* Methodical approach to work.
* Strong understanding of medical and clinical research terminology, and clinical research processes.
* An understanding of the basics of physiology, pharmacology, and medical devices (when applicable).
* Strong understanding of the principles of regulatory requirements.
* Good computer literacy with working knowledge of PCs, Windows, and Microsoft Office applications.
* Good oral and written internal and external communication. Strong interpersonal team and organizational skills, personal presentation. The ability to communicate effectively in English.
* Experience using a clinical trial management system (CTMS).
* For medical device positions, experience in providing customer service to device end users.

**Physical Demands/Work Environment:**

* Travel Requirement: Yes

T he base salary range for this role is € 47,800 \- 63,000 gross per year, which may vary as per working schedule (full or part\-time). The final offer will be determined based on objective and job\-related criteria such as experience, qualifications, and scope of responsibilities, in line with our internal job classification framework and the applicable collective labour agreement (CCNL). This role may also be eligible for additional compensation, such as bonus and benefits, in accordance with company policies


Learn more about our EEO \& Accommodations request here .

title: Campus manager Milan
description:
Led by an experienced management team and supported by a strong investor group, including large and experienced institutions and strategic partners, EdgeConneX offers a dynamic, fast\-paced work environment where we are bringing flexibility, proximity, power, and connectivity to some of the world's key businesses. With major offices in Herndon, Denver, and Amsterdam, we have a global footprint and a unified team of employees committed to providing a premier customer experience and delivering the full spectrum of data center solutions, from core to edge, like no other data center provider can do.



Focused on driving innovation and helping our customers define and deliver their own unique vision for the Edge, at any scale, in any market worldwide, for any requirement, we are building tomorrow's data center infrastructure, today for some of the world's most demanding Network, Content, and Cloud customers.


The Campus Manager serves as the on\-site operational leader for the assigned data centre campus, with overall responsibility for translating direction from the Director of Operations into consistent, disciplined daily execution. This role leads a team of site engineers and skilled technical personnel, oversees the application of company policies and procedures at site level, and identifies opportunities to improve operational processes within the campus or functional discipline. The position is accountable for maintaining site uptime, meeting SLA commitments, delivering preventive and corrective maintenance programmes, and ensuring effective vendor oversight and performance.



This role requires onsite training/onboarding in one or multiple other EdgeConneX locations. Expected travel in the first 6 months is significant.


**Primary Responsibilities**


* Lead and supervise the local site engineering team, including task allocation, work oversight, performance management, and capability development in line with EdgeConneX policies.
* Translate operational objectives from the Director of Operations into structured site plans, schedules, and day\-to\-day workflows.
* Own and deliver the site preventive maintenance programme, while acting as the on\-site lead for corrective and emergency maintenance activities.
* Implement and oversee company policies and procedures at site level, recommending improvements to local processes where opportunities are identified.
* Manage the CMMS to ensure work orders, asset records, and maintenance histories are complete, accurate, and up to date.
* Supervise third\-party contractors and vendors, ensuring compliance with approved Methods of Procedures (MOPs) and EdgeConneX operational standards.
* Maintain 100% uptime and ensure full compliance with customer SLAs, acting as the primary escalation point for operational issues at site level.
* Monitor and manage site operating costs, identifying and escalating variances to the Director of Operations as appropriate.
* Conduct regular assessments of building systems and recommend improvements to operational efficiency, reliability, and PUE performance.
* Ensure compliance with all applicable safety, environmental, and site regulatory requirements, promoting a strong culture of operational safety.
* Support site infrastructure expansion and MEP upgrade projects by coordinating with PMO, Engineering, and other cross\-functional teams.


**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**


* Experience supervising professional or skilled technical teams, including assigning work, tracking progress, and reviewing performance.
* Ability to interpret management direction and established processes and convert them into clear operational plans.
* Strong understanding of data centre electrical and mechanical systems, including UPS, PDU, generators, HV/LV switchgear, CRAC/CRAH units, and chillers.
* Practical experience leading preventive and corrective maintenance programmes in a mission\-critical environment.
* Proficient in CMMS systems and work order management.
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical information clearly to team members and peers.
* Able to build effective working relationships with contractors, vendors, and cross\-functional teams.
* Fluency in both the local language and English is required.


**Experience \& Education**


* Minimum of 8 years' experience in critical facilities operations, maintenance, or engineering environments, including at least 3–5 years in a supervisory, team lead, or management capacity.
* Proven track record of leading and developing professional and skilled technical teams, with direct responsibility for personnel management and performance.
* Bachelor's degree in Applied Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent combination of education and relevant industry experience.
* Valid HSE certification ( or equivalent local occupational health and safety qualification) required; OSHA 30 certification preferred or to be obtained within a specified period following hire.
* Strong understanding of local regulatory requirements, utility infrastructure, and electrical grid operations is highly desirable.


*The starting salary will be no less than €43,601\.88 gross per year and the final offer will be fine tuned based on gender neutral criteria of candidates, including but not limited to competencies, responsibilities, effort and work conditions.*



EdgeConneX believes inclusion – of thought, backgrounds and experiences – affects all that we do, from our employees to the solutions we deliver. Our goal is to create an environment where embracing differences helps deepen the lives and work experience of our employees, enhances our innovation and creativity, and enriches our involvement in our communities. EdgeConneX is an equal employment opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin or ancestry, physical or mental disability, as well as any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.



EdgeConneX offers a competitive benefits package.



For more information on how we process your data, visit our Data Privacy Policy here.

title: PDS QA Project Specialist
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**SCOPO DELLA POSIZIONE/RESPONSABILITÀ:**

* Alle dipendenze del QA Ops Team Leader di riferimento, supporta la Persona Qualificata nel giudizio sui lotti prodotti e sull’ambiente di lavorazione, attraverso la verifica dei certificati di analisi e delle altre informazioni necessarie; effettua valutazione di impatto preliminare di eventi di deviazione e ne revisiona i report di indagine; si occupa di raccolta di reclami da parte dei clienti e revisione delle indagini investigative nel rispetto degli standard, delle procedure aziendali e delle normative vigenti (leggi, norme GMP, FDA, etc.).

**ATTIVITÀ PRIMARIE:**

**Controllo lotti per rilascio**

* Supportare la QP e il Quality Operations Manager nel rilascio dei lotti di prodotto, assicurando la conformità alle specifiche attraverso la verifica documentale dei certificati di analisi, la raccolta e l’analisi dei batch record eseguiti, il controllo dello stato di deviazioni e change control pertinenti al lotto.
* Assicurare la corretta archiviazione di tutta la documentazione relativa ai batch record nel rispetto degli standard e delle procedure aziendali.

**Revisione record di indagine**

* Eseguire valutazione preliminare di eventi di deviazioni e di reclami, assegnare il livello di criticalità iniziale e valutare l’eventuale necessità di escalation al management.
* Revisionare il piano di investigazione, supportare l’investigator nell’identificazione di root cause e azioni correttive e preventive.
* Revisione completa delle indagini di deviazioni e reclami (es. materiale a supporto dell’indagine, impatto di qualità su lotti/aree coinvolte, impatto regolatorio e di compliance).
* Contribuire al raggiungimento degli obbiettivi Zero Past Due per la propria area di competenza e degli obbiettivi RFT e OTD.
* Approvazione delle azioni preventive e correttive e dei record di verifica di efficacia nel tempo delle stesse.

**Gestione Cliente e Ispezioni**

* Partecipare a riunioni periodiche con cliente e fornire supporto via email rispondendo ai quesiti di qualità per la propria area di competenza
* Notificare evento di deviazione e reclami a cliente in accordo a QTA e garantirne la chiusura dei record in Trackwise.
* Redigere KPI dedicati per clienti e partecipare a visite cliente in sito
* Partecipare attivamente nella preparazione di Audit
* Supportare nella presentazione di argomenti di competenza durante audit

**Compliance**

* Assicurare l’esecuzione dei training assegnati al proprio profilo
* Assicurare il continuo aggiornamento delle SOP riguardanti l’area di competenza, attraverso la redazione della documentazione necessaria, in conformità ai requisiti di GMP vigenti.
* Revisionare i MBR dei prodotti legati a progetti PDS (lotti clinici e commerciali)
* Revisionare i change control legati a progetti PDS
* Revisionare protocolli e report relativi ai lotti clinici
* Redigere PSF relativi ai lotti clinici in accordo alle SOP vigenti e garantirne il continuo aggiornamento
* Emettere Risk Assessment legati a progetti PDS per la propria area di competenza
* Revisionare FMEA legate a progetti PDS

**RAPPORTI:**

**INTERNI**

* Produzione
* QA Compliance e Regulatory/QC
* Business Manager e PMO
* Servizi Tecnici
* Logistica

**ESTERNI**

* Clienti\- Enti Regolatori

**REQUISITI E QUALIFICHE:**

**ESSENZIALI**

**Studi/Diploma/Corsi di formazione:**

* Laurea in CTF, Farmacia, Chimica, Biologia o analoghe lauree a indirizzo scientifico

**Conoscenze tecniche e informatiche:**

* Produzione e controllo della produzione di prodotti farmaceutici (clinici e commerciali)
* Norme GMP/FDA

**Lingue:**

* Inglese

**Tratti di personalità:**

* Capacità di lavorare in team
* Capacità organizzative
* Ottime doti relazionali
* Problem solving

**DESIDERABILI**

* Laurea in CTF, Chimica, Farmacia, biologia, biotecnologia o campo scientifico correlato

title: Sales Intern - Italy (d/m/f)
description:
#### **Your Responsibilities**


As a Sales Intern, you can expect to:

* Help keep the Italian sales pipeline moving by preparing accurate, client\-ready offers and commercial proposals.
* Contribute to the professionalism of Entrix's commercial offering in Italy by maintaining our sales decks, one\-pagers, and enablement materials to a high standard.
* Translate market and asset data into structured, client\-facing inputs, working closely with our Market Expert, the Ops and Tech Team.
* Understand pricing logic, project parameters, and regulatory context well enough to keep offers and proposal accurate.
* Keep HubSpot precise and current, so the sales team always has a reliable picture of the pipeline.
* Track follow\-ups, deadlines, and outstanding requests, keeping the team ahead of its commitments.

#### **Your Profile**


We're looking for someone who is deeply precise and accountable, someone who doesn't just execute a task but checks it, catches what's off, and takes ownership of getting the details right.

**Requirements**

* You have studied business administration, economics, engineering, or a related field, or at least in your last year of a Master's.
* C1 level proficiency in English and Italian.
* Strong attention to detail and a methodical, structured way of working, even under multiple simultaneous requests.
* Comfortable working with HubSpot or a similar CRM (or a fast learner on new systems).
* Ability to understand technical and commercial inputs, pricing logic, project parameters, regulatory context well enough to translate them accurately into client\-facing material.
* Excellent written and visual communication skills, particularly for professional and commercial documents.
* Comfortable coordinating across the sales, market, and technical teams.

**Skills to Set You Apart**

* Prior experience with HubSpot or another CRM platform.
* Experience preparing commercial offers, proposals, or sales decks.
* An understanding of the Italian energy landscape, including renewables, grid\-scale storage, or power trading.
* Strong command of Excel and PowerPoint/Google Slides and Claude.
* Exposure to B2B sales or business development processes.

**This role may not be a fit for you if…**

* You prefer conceptual, big\-picture work over hands\-on, detail\-driven execution.
* You are not comfortable double\-checking your own work or following structured processes.
* You are not interested in the energy landscape or in understanding technical/regulatory detail.
* You are uncomfortable coordinating across multiple stakeholders and teams.


At Entrix, we are committed to diversity \- of all backgrounds and experiences. You don't need 100% of the preferred qualifications to add incredible value to our team. If you're passionate about what you could accomplish here, we'd love to hear from you.#### **A Short Note from Your Future Lead**

**Michele Pissarello \- Head of Country Italy \-** **LinkedIn**


Hello future colleague,


My name is Michele, and I am the Country Manager for Italy at Entrix. I'm excited to build and grow our Italian team and look forward to receiving your application.


I bring over 15 years of global experience in the energy storage sector, having worked across a range of roles—from technical support and project execution to business development and sales—with companies like Enel, Tesla, and Toshiba. Most recently, I led the Business Development team at Enel, where we developed and executed over 1 GW of storage assets worldwide, both front\-of\-the\-meter and behind\-the\-meter.


At Entrix, we're building something ambitious—a smart, tech\-driven platform designed to unlock the full value of battery storage in energy markets. As our commercial activity in Italy scales, precision matters more, not less: every offer, every CRM record, every deck needs to hold up to technical and regulatory scrutiny. That's what this role is for.


This position is a unique opportunity to get a perspective on both the sales and business development work we do at Entrix — you'll work directly with our Market Expert to turn market and asset data into material our clients can act on, and you'll see a deal's full lifecycle from first offer to onboarding and go\-live. It rewards someone who's precise, structured, and genuinely curious about how the numbers and the regulation behind a deal actually work.


Looking forward to what we'll create together.


Best regards,


Michele

#### **Your Contact in our People Team**


Your contact person is Varsha Singh. If you have any questions, feel free to send us an email to kalyan\-varsha.singh@entrixenergy.com.  

We are looking forward to receiving your application!#### **About us**

#### **We are an energy trader and market leader for trading flexible energy systems in Europe.**

  

Beyond AI\-powered trading, Entrix provides innovative solutions for long\-term revenue security and grid\-supportive battery operation. With our full\-service package, we support customers throughout every project phase – from technical design and integration, to continuous optimization, all the way to 24/7 operational support. That includes adapting to changing market conditions and unlocking new revenue opportunities.  

  

With more than 2\.3 GW and 38 battery storage assets under contract, Entrix is active in Germany, Poland, Italy, Spain, and Portugal, with offices in Munich, Warsaw, Milan, and Madrid. Since 2022, Entrix has been pioneering battery optimization in Germany by managing one of the country’s first large\-scale storage projects – and is now the trusted partner of leading infrastructure players such as Encavis, MEAG (Munich Re), as well as a wide range of local utilities.

title: Junior Brand Manager Multicentrum & Digestive
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.
  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.
  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.


**About the role**

As Junior Brand Manager Multicentrum \& Digestive you will support the Italian VMS marketing team to develop and implement several activations and ensure our growth over the next years.


**Key Responsibilities**

In this role you will have the opportunities to improve your marketing skills through different activities:


* Track performance (market, competition, brands, consumers), monitor and analyze sell\-out data, and focus on learning to recommend strategies and actions based on data
* Manage and support the team on media and communications and be point of contact for others involved departments (Regulatory, Medical, Legal)
* Actively work on channel specific activations (focus on DComm channel and future opportunities)
* Support the team on 3\-years marketing plan and activities implementation
* Monitor and be in charge of A\&P (Advertising\&Promotion spending)
* Work in cooperation with Trade marketing, Finance and Sales teams on promotional activations

**Qualifications \& Skills**

* University degree in Business or related
* At least 6 months of experience in Marketing department
* Fluent in English and Italian
* Passion for Brand Management and Healthcare
* Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
* Passion for data analytics
* Ability to manage complexity with multiple projects
Proactive, positive and able to create collaborative relationships  
* 

\&\#xa;EUR 32\.126,00 \- EUR 44\.173,80\&\#xa;\&\#xa;The salary range for this position is indicative and provided as a guide. The final offer may vary depending on factors including relevant experience, skills, qualifications, and internal equity considerations. Further details regarding the total reward and benefits package will be shared during the recruitment process. Salary ranges may vary by location in line with local market conditions and applicable pay transparency regulations.\&\#xa;\&\#xa;Compensation information is shared for the primary location of this role. Where the position is advertised with additional locations the compensation range will be discussed during the recruiting process.  

**Job Posting End Date**

2026\-07\-18  

**Equal Opportunities**
-----------------------

Haleon are committed to mobilising our purpose in a way that represents the diverse consumers and communities who rely on our brands every day. It guides us in creating an inclusive culture, where different backgrounds and views are valued and respected – all in support of understanding and best serving the needs of our consumers and unleashing the full potential of our people. It’s important to us that Haleon is a place where all our employees feel they truly belong.
  

  

During the application process, we may ask you to share some personal information, which is entirely voluntary. This information ensures we meet certain regulatory and reporting obligations and supports the development, refinement, and execution of our inclusion and belonging programmes that are open to all Haleon employees.
  

  

The personal information you provide will be kept confidential, used only for legitimate business purposes, and will never be used in making any employment decisions, including hiring decisions.  



**Adjustment or Accommodations Request**
----------------------------------------

If you require a reasonable adjustment or accommodation or other assistance to apply for a job at Haleon at any stage of the application process, please let your recruiter know by providing them with a description of specific adjustments you are requesting. We’ll provide all reasonable adjustments to support you throughout the recruitment process and treat all information you provide us in confidence.  



**Note to candidates**
----------------------

The Haleon recruitment team will contact you using a Haleon email account (@haleon.com). If you are not sure whether the email you received is from Haleon, please get in touch.

title: Senior Project Manager
description:
At Unispace, we create spaces that empower people and businesses to thrive. We are a global design\-build partner delivering workplace and life sciences projects for some of the world's most innovative brands.



With over 500 people across four continents, we bring together strategy, design, project management, and delivery into one seamless experience. Our integrated approach enables us to move with agility, solve complex challenges, and deliver meaningful outcomes for our clients.



Our people are at the centre of everything we do. We foster a collaborative, inclusive culture that values curiosity, accountability, creativity, and continuous improvement. We believe diverse perspectives strengthen our teams and help us create better experiences for our clients and each other.



At Unispace, you'll have the opportunity to contribute to impactful projects, work alongside talented global teams, and grow your career in an environment that encourages innovation and new ideas.

**Role Profile**  

As a Senior Project Manager (SPM) in our Delivery team, you will play an influential role in overseeing complex design and construction projects, building strong client relationships, and leading project teams. This includes responsibility for the day\-to\-day management of large\-scale project elements and managing multiple Fit\-out projects at different stages simultaneously, ensuring a focus on high\-quality, client\- focused project delivery.


**Responsibilities include, but not limited to:**


* Leading large\-scale, complex projects from initial planning and conception through to final close\-out, ensuring designs are completed on time and in line with client budgets.
* Serving as the primary client contact, building and maintaining strong, trusting relationships, while proactively tailoring project strategies to exceed expectations.
* Managing and coordinating all project aspects, including budgeting, scheduling, resource allocation, risk management, and regulatory compliance.
* Chairing regular client and stakeholder meetings, providing transparent progress updates, addressing concerns, and fostering open, solution\-focused communication.
* Overseeing all subcontractor and vendor relationships, evaluating tender bids, and ensuring performance standards align with strict quality benchmarks.
* Leading site inspections and coordinating with Site Superintendents and trade partners to monitor construction quality, solve on\-site challenges, and maintain rigorous health and safety compliance.
* Collaborating closely across internal departments—including design, engineering, and pre\-construction teams—to ensure seamless integration of client requirements and firm standards.
* Developing and implementing project management best practices, enhancing documentation, and mentoring junior project managers and project engineers to foster professional growth.
* Managing overall project financials, including comprehensive budgeting, precise forecasting, and continuous cost control to ensure all financial goals are met.


**Your skills and experience:**


* Experience \& Expertise: 10\+ years of proven project management experience within the fit\-out construction field, ideally with demonstrated involvement in medium and large\-scale projects.
* Client Focus: A relationship\-focused professional driven to provide an outstanding experience through proactive communication, exceptional service, and an innate ability to anticipate client priorities.
* Technical \& Regulatory knowledge: Strong understanding of commercial fit\-out methodologies, local regulatory requirements, trade coordination, and health \& safety systems.
* Systems Proficiency: Experienced in the use of Microsoft Project, Vista, or other construction PM tools and scheduling software tools.
* Commercial knowledge: Solid commercial acumen with comprehensive experience in project budgeting, forecasting, transparent cost reporting, and contract administration.
* Leadership Culture: An organized, detail\-oriented strategic thinker who inspires team accountability, thrives under tight timelines, and passionately champions an inclusive, supportive workplace culture.


**What's next?**



If you are ready to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of global workplaces and want to join an innovative culture where your voice is heard, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and outlining your experience in leading fit\-out projects.  

Our recruitment team will review your application, and qualified candidates will be contacted for an initial introductory conversation, followed by formal panel interviews with our Delivery leadership team.


The statements in this job description are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by individuals assigned to this role. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, or skills required.



We encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds and experiences and are committed to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the recruitment process and employment lifecycle where required by applicable law.


**Inclusion \& Diversity**
--------------------------



At Unispace, we believe diverse perspectives strengthen our teams, our culture, and the work we deliver for our clients. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where everyone is treated with respect and has the opportunity to thrive.



Employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business needs, in accordance with applicable local laws and regulations.
